question_frequency.rationale: Only one explicit forking question (A vs B for counting approach) and a follow-up offer to explore. Most teachable moments (zip truncation, bool-as-int) are stated rather than asked.

question_quality.rationale: The single A/B choice is largely cosmetic — both options are correct and differ only in style. It doesn't surface the real crux (why guard must precede zip, or empty-string semantics).

scaffolding.rationale: Minimal scaffolding: one stylistic choice, then solution written. The zip-truncation insight is delivered as a statement after the choice, not built up through questions.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is concise and warm, conversation flows naturally, and follow-ups are actionable (offer to run tests). Good back-and-forth without friction.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is identical in substance to the baseline and passes the standard Exercism test suite that was executed inline.

friction.rationale: Three turns vs baseline's two, ~65s vs ~35s, with modest extra value (running tests, surfacing zip-truncation gotcha proactively). Roughly proportional.
